sections.title: Section A: Multiple-Choice Questions
sections.description: Answer all questions. Choose the correct letter (A-D).

sections.title: Section B: Structured Questions
sections.description: Answer all questions in the spaces provided.

questions.text: Which ONE of the following organelles is responsible for the synthesis of proteins in a eukaryotic cell?
The diagram below represents a plant tissue. Identify the tissue labelled X.
Define the term homeostasis and explain why it is important for enzyme-controlled reactions.
questions.answer: Homeostasis is the maintenance of a constant internal environment, ensuring optimal conditions (e.g., temperature and pH) for enzyme activity.

questions.text: Study the graph provided and describe how insulin and glucagon maintain blood glucose concentration after a carbohydrate-rich meal.
questions.answer: Insulin is secreted when blood glucose rises; it stimulates glucose uptake and glycogenesis. Glucagon is secreted when glucose drops; it stimulates glycogenolysis to increase blood glucose.

questions.text: Discuss the adaptations of the human nephron that make ultrafiltration, selective reabsorption, and secretion efficient processes.
